name: COTS Detroit – Youth Housing
eligibility: Youth ages 16–24 experiencing homelessness or housing instability
intake_process: Call ahead or walk in during business hours. ID preferred but not required.

name: Gleaners Community Food Bank – Wayne County
eligibility: Open to all Wayne County residents in need
intake_process: Visit the website to find the nearest distribution site. No appointment needed at most locations.

name: Detroit Wayne Integrated Health Network – Mental Health Crisis Line
eligibility: Any Wayne County resident experiencing a mental health or substance use crisis
intake_process: Call the crisis line at any time. Walk-in crisis services available at select locations.

name: Michigan Legal Help – Wayne County Clinic
eligibility: Low-income residents; income eligibility based on household size
intake_process: Call or visit the website to schedule a free legal consultation or access self-help resources.

name: Covenant Community Care – Detroit Health Clinic
eligibility: All patients accepted regardless of ability to pay; sliding fee scale available
intake_process: Walk-in or call for an appointment. Sliding scale fees based on income. Uninsured patients welcome.

name: Monroe County Community Action Agency
eligibility: Monroe County residents experiencing housing insecurity or homelessness
intake_process: Call during business hours to schedule an intake appointment. Documentation of need is helpful.

name: Monroe County Food Pantry Network
hours: Varies by location; most open Tue and Thu: 10am–2pm
eligibility: Monroe County residents; identification helpful but not always required
intake_process: Find the nearest pantry on the website. Most allow walk-in access during posted hours.

name: Ozone House – Youth Drop-In Center
eligibility: Youth ages 14–24 in crisis or at risk
intake_process: Walk in during drop-in hours. No appointment needed. Crisis line available 24/7.

name: Wayne Metro Community Action Agency – Emergency Utility Assistance
eligibility: Wayne County residents with household income at or below 200% of federal poverty level
intake_process: Call to schedule intake. Bring proof of income, ID, and recent utility bills.

name: Monroe County Health Department – Youth Clinic
eligibility: Monroe County youth and residents; some services income-based
intake_process: Call for an appointment. Immunizations and some services available as walk-in.

name: National Runaway Safeline
eligibility: Youth under 21 who have run away, are considering running away, or are experiencing homelessness
intake_process: Call 1-800-RUNAWAY (786-2929) or chat online at 1800runaway.org. The Home Free program provides free bus tickets to help unaccompanied youth reconnect with family.

name: Covenant House Michigan
address: 2959 Martin Luther King Jr Blvd, Detroit, MI 48208
eligibility: Youth ages 17–24 experiencing homelessness or housing instability in the Detroit area
intake_process: Walk in or call 24/7 for crisis intake. Programs include emergency shelter, transitional housing, street outreach, and physical and mental health services.

name: Hope Clinic
address: Multiple locations – visit website for nearest site
hours: Varies by location; call or visit website for current hours
eligibility: Uninsured and underinsured patients; free non-emergency primary care for Wayne County and surrounding areas
intake_process: Call or visit the website to find a location. Services include free primary care, limited specialty care, health assessments, behavioral health, food resources, and MI Bridges/Healthy Michigan enrollment assistance.

name: Ruth Ellis Center
eligibility: LGBTQ+ youth and young adults ages 13–30 in the Detroit metro area
intake_process: Call or visit the website. Services include housing, outreach, health and wellness, art therapy, family preservation, positive youth development, and juvenile justice support.

name: Detroit Phoenix Center
eligibility: Detroit youth and young adults; education, employment, and housing support available
intake_process: Visit website or call. Services include education and employment resources, emergency housing support, youth leadership action board, and a drop-in center. Also offers laundry, showers, computer lab, food pantry, case management, and mental health support.

name: Capuchin Soup Kitchen
hours: Mon–Sat: 7am–9am and 11am–1pm (meals); pantry and service center hours vary
eligibility: Open to all Detroit residents in need; no ID required for meals
intake_process: Walk in during meal service hours. The Shoppers' Choice food and clothing pantry lets guests select items free of charge. Furniture referrals available through the service center.

name: The Children Center
eligibility: Children, youth, and families in the Detroit metro area; sliding scale fees; Medicaid accepted
intake_process: Call to schedule an intake appointment. Provides clinical, behavioral, and academic support as well as emergency crisis care for Detroit's most vulnerable children.

name: SAY Detroit Family Health Clinic
eligibility: All adults and families regardless of ability to pay; sliding scale available; on-site pharmacy
intake_process: Call to schedule an appointment or walk in. Provides high-quality examinations, preventive healthcare, and maintenance care with an on-site pharmacy.

name: New Oakland Family Centers
address: Multiple Southeast Michigan locations – see website
eligibility: Children, teens, and families in Southeast Michigan; specializes in youth mental health; accepts most insurance and Medicaid
intake_process: Call to schedule an intake evaluation. Mental health services for children, teens, and families with specialty programs available at multiple locations.

name: Lighthouse Michigan
eligibility: Youth ages 10–17 (shelter program), adults and families in Oakland and surrounding counties; housing assistance, emergency shelter, and homelessness prevention
intake_process: Call or visit during business hours. Shelter program available for runaways and unaccompanied youth ages 10–17. Also offers affordable housing units when available and drop-in programs.

name: Joyful Treats
eligibility: Detroit youth interested in entrepreneurship in the catering and hospitality sector; an alternative career pathway to college
intake_process: Visit the website for program details, application information, and upcoming events.

name: Youth Up
eligibility: Youth and young adults in Detroit needing wraparound support services
intake_process: Visit the website or drop in during posted hours. Services include laundry, showers, computer lab, food pantry, case management, basic needs, emergency shelter, mental health and wellness, youth leadership, and education and employment support.

name: Dufree Innovation Society
eligibility: Detroit youth and community members seeking free academic and counseling support
intake_process: Walk in or visit the website. Provides a free laundry room, tutoring services, library and homework rooms, and counseling services.

name: The Youth Connection
hours: Visit website for current hours and program schedules
eligibility: Youth and families in the Detroit area
intake_process: Visit the website or call for program enrollment. Programs include career and college prep, fitness and nutrition classes, substance abuse prevention, after-school fairs, and the Strengthening Families Program.

name: Grow Detroit's Young Talent
hours: Program runs summer months; applications open in early spring
eligibility: Detroit youth ages 14–24; must live, work, or attend school in Detroit
intake_process: Apply online at gdyt.org during the application window. Provides meaningful paid summer employment opportunities and connects youth to local employers.

name: Michigan Works! Association
address: Multiple locations statewide – visit website for nearest office
eligibility: Michigan residents seeking employment, job training, or credentials; youth and adult programs available
intake_process: Visit your nearest Michigan Works! service center or the website. Services include job search support, resume help, training programs, credentials, and connections to employers.

name: SER Metro-Detroit
eligibility: Detroit and Metro Detroit residents; adult and youth programs available including YouthBuild for trade skills and GED prep
intake_process: Call or visit to schedule an intake. Programs include adult work readiness, job placement, YouthBuild trade skills training, and GED preparation.

name: Detroit Job Corps Center
eligibility: Youth ages 16–24; free program including housing, meals, and stipend for eligible participants
intake_process: Call or visit the website to apply. Free services include high school diploma (via Penn Foster), high-paying trade programs (CNA, CMA, HVAC, etc.), three meals daily, bi-weekly stipend, childcare, driver's training, on-center medical care, transportation, and counseling.

name: Midwest Access Coalition
eligibility: Individuals in Michigan and the Midwest seeking abortion access and reproductive healthcare support
intake_process: Contact via the website. Provides practical support for abortion access and care, including navigation, logistics, and financial assistance.

name: Oaks Shelter
address: Contact via Wayne County for referral information
eligibility: Detroit residents referred through Wayne County social services or partner organizations
intake_process: Access through a referral from a caseworker or partner agency. Contact Wayne Metro or COTS for a referral.

name: Covenant House Michigan – Grand Rapids
address: 425 Sheldon Ave SE, Grand Rapids, MI 49503
eligibility: Youth ages 17–24 experiencing homelessness or housing instability in the Grand Rapids area
intake_process: Walk in or call 24/7. Services include emergency shelter, crisis support, street outreach, and physical and mental health services.

name: National Human Trafficking Hotline
eligibility: Anyone experiencing or reporting human trafficking
intake_process: Call or text 233733 (HELP or INFO) for immediate assistance and referrals

name: Michigan VOICES4 Hotline
eligibility: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, sexual assault, or trafficking
intake_process: Call, text, or chat via website for confidential support

name: Sanctum House
eligibility: Adult female survivors of human trafficking
intake_process: Referral-based admission through partner organizations

name: Hope Against Trafficking
eligibility: Adult female survivors of trafficking
intake_process: Apply or contact through website for placement

name: Turning Point (Macomb County)
address: 36660 S Gratiot Ave, Clinton Twp, MI
eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, and trafficking
intake_process: Call hotline for immediate shelter or services

name: Avalon Healing Center
eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault and trafficking
intake_process: Call hotline or walk in for services

name: Alternatives For Girls
eligibility: At-risk and homeless young women and trafficking survivors
intake_process: Call or visit website for program enrollment

name: Global Alliance Solutions Foundation
eligibility: Survivors of trafficking, especially immigrants
intake_process: Call or submit request through website

name: Sparrow Freedom Project
eligibility: Survivors of trafficking and exploitation
intake_process: Contact via phone or website for services

name: University of Michigan Human Trafficking Clinic
address: 625 S State St, Ann Arbor, MI
eligibility: Survivors of human trafficking seeking legal assistance
intake_process: Submit request through website

name: CAM Detroit – Community Resource Navigator
eligibility: Anyone in the Detroit metro area seeking community resources
intake_process: Browse the online directory or contact CAM Detroit directly through their website

name: HUDA Clinic
eligibility: Uninsured and underinsured individuals
intake_process: Call or visit website to schedule appointment

name: Hope Clinic – Westland
eligibility: Uninsured or low-income individuals
intake_process: Call or register online for services

name: Gary Burnstein Community Health Clinic
eligibility: Uninsured adults
intake_process: Call to schedule appointment

name: MyCare Health Center
eligibility: Accepts Medicaid, uninsured, and insured patients
intake_process: Call or schedule online

name: Covenant Community Care – Grand Blvd
eligibility: Medicaid, uninsured, sliding scale
intake_process: Call or visit website to schedule

name: CHASS Center
eligibility: Medicaid and uninsured patients
intake_process: Call or schedule online

name: My Community Dental Centers
eligibility: Accepts Medicaid and low-income patients
intake_process: Call to schedule appointment

name: Planned Parenthood – Detroit Health Center
eligibility: Accepts Medicaid, uninsured, sliding scale
intake_process: Call or book online

name: Community Mental Health (Wayne County)
eligibility: Medicaid and eligible residents
intake_process: Call access line for assessment

name: Community First Health Centers
eligibility: Medicaid, uninsured, sliding scale
intake_process: Call or schedule online

name: Detroit Recovery Project
eligibility: Individuals seeking substance use recovery support
intake_process: Call or walk in

name: ACCESS Community Health & Research Center
eligibility: Medicaid, uninsured, community members
intake_process: Call to schedule

name: CVS Pharmacy – Low Cost Prescriptions
eligibility: General public
intake_process: Visit pharmacy or transfer prescription

name: Henry Ford Health – Same Day Care
eligibility: Accepts Medicaid and most insurance; self-pay options available
intake_process: Walk-in or reserve spot online

name: Get Well Urgent Care
eligibility: Accepts Medicaid, uninsured, and insured patients
intake_process: Walk-in or online check-in

name: Detroit Health Department – Vision Services
eligibility: Detroit residents, Medicaid and uninsured
intake_process: Call for appointment or referral

name: Vision To Learn (Mobile Vision Care)
eligibility: Children and youth, low-income families
intake_process: Through schools or community programs

name: Good Samaritan Free Clinic (Pontiac)
eligibility: Uninsured and low-income individuals
intake_process: Call to confirm eligibility and schedule

name: Michigan Medicine Community Health Services – Screening Programs
eligibility: Low-income and underserved populations
intake_process: Call or attend outreach events

name: St. Vincent de Paul Free Pharmacy
eligibility: Uninsured and low-income individuals
intake_process: Referral required from clinic or provider

name: Catholic Charities of Southeast Michigan – Counseling Services
eligibility: Open to all; sliding scale available
intake_process: Call or request services online

name: Islamic House of Wisdom – Counseling & Community Support
eligibility: Open community access
intake_process: Call or visit for services

name: Southwest Detroit Immigrant & Refugee Center
eligibility: Immigrants, refugees, asylum seekers
intake_process: Walk-in clinic, call, or online consultation

name: Michigan Immigrant Rights Center
eligibility: Low-income immigrants
intake_process: Call intake line for screening

name: Immigration Law & Justice Michigan
eligibility: Low-income immigrants and refugees
intake_process: Appointment required

name: Wayne State Immigration Law Clinic
eligibility: Michigan residents seeking immigration relief
intake_process: Apply through website

name: USCRI Detroit
eligibility: Refugees and immigrants
intake_process: Call or email for services

name: International Institute of Metropolitan Detroit
eligibility: Immigrants and refugees
intake_process: Schedule consultation

name: Michigan United
eligibility: Immigrant communities
intake_process: Call or email for programs

name: Freedom House Detroit
eligibility: Asylum seekers and refugees
intake_process: Call for intake

name: Migrant Legal Aid
eligibility: Migrant and seasonal workers
intake_process: Contact through website

name: Lighthouse Immigrant Advocates
eligibility: Immigrant communities and their families
intake_process: Call for legal advocacy and immigration support
description: Provides immigrant advocacy, legal referrals, and community support in West Michigan.

name: Justice for Our Neighbors
address: 207 Fulton St E, Grand Rapids, MI 49503
eligibility: Immigrants and refugees seeking legal help
intake_process: Call for immigration legal services and advocacy referrals
description: Legal aid organization offering immigration advocacy and services in Grand Rapids.

name: Immigration Legal Services
address: 123 Wealthy St SE, Grand Rapids, MI 49503
eligibility: Immigrants needing legal counseling
intake_process: Call to schedule immigration legal advice and support
description: Provides immigration legal services and advocacy in Grand Rapids.

name: Heart of West Michigan United Way
address: 118 Commerce Ave SW, Grand Rapids, MI 49503
eligibility: Residents needing community resource referrals
intake_process: Call or dial 2-1-1 for immigrant service referrals
description: United Way resource hub that connects immigrants to local services through 2-1-1 referrals.

name: Migrant Resource Councils – Michigan
eligibility: Migrants, farmworkers, and migrant families
intake_process: Call or email Audra Fuentes for advocacy and resource connections
description: Statewide migrant resource council providing advocacy and support for farmworkers and migrant families.

name: Michigan Interagency Migrant Service Committee
eligibility: Migrants and immigrant-serving organizations
intake_process: Call or email Audra Fuentes for migrant service coordination
description: Statewide interagency committee supporting migrant service coordination and advocacy.

name: North Kent Connect
eligibility: Immigrant families and individuals in North Kent County
intake_process: Call for immigration referrals, community support, and advocacy
description: Community resource center connecting immigrants in North Kent County to support services.

name: Hispanic Center of West Michigan
address: 1204 Cesar E Chavez Ave SW, Grand Rapids, MI 49503
eligibility: Hispanic and immigrant communities
intake_process: Call for community programs, advocacy, and immigration referrals
description: Community center offering immigrant advocacy, referrals, and legal guidance in West Michigan.

name: Refugee Education Center
eligibility: Refugees and immigrant learners
intake_process: Call for educational programs and refugee support
description: Provides education, refugee support, and advocacy services to immigrant families in Kentwood.

name: Michigan Coalition for Immigrant and Refugee Rights
eligibility: Immigrants, refugees, and immigrant rights advocates
intake_process: Visit the website for contact details and advocacy resources
description: Statewide coalition advocating for immigrant and refugee rights in Michigan.

name: Immigration Advocates Network Legal Directory
eligibility: Immigrants, community advocates, and legal aid organizations
intake_process: Use the website to search nonprofit immigration legal service providers by location and specialty
description: National nonprofit directory of nonprofit immigration legal service providers, including Michigan-based organizations and immigrant advocacy groups.

name: First Step
address: Confidential shelter location / administrative offices in Plymouth area
eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence and sexual violence in Wayne County
intake_process: Call the 24-hour helpline for crisis support, referrals, shelter access, advocacy, and other services.
description: Wayne County provider offering crisis intervention, shelter, advocacy, referrals, and supportive services for survivors.

name: HAVEN
eligibility: Survivors of intimate partner violence and sexual assault in Oakland County
intake_process: Call the crisis line for immediate support, shelter access, advocacy, and referrals.
description: Oakland County provider offering comprehensive support for survivors of intimate partner violence and sexual assault.

name: Turning Point
eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual violence, and human trafficking in Macomb County
intake_process: Call the hotline for crisis support, shelter, advocacy, counseling, housing support, and referrals.
description: Macomb County provider offering free and confidential support including hotline, shelter, counseling, and advocacy.

name: SafeHouse Center
address: Confidential shelter location / Ann Arbor area
eligibility: Survivors who live, work, or were victimized in Washtenaw County
intake_process: Call the helpline for support, referrals, counseling, legal advocacy, and emergency shelter access.
description: Washtenaw County provider offering free and confidential services including shelter, support, advocacy, and referrals.

name: Michigan VOICES4 Hotline
eligibility: Anyone in Michigan seeking support related to domestic violence, sexual assault, or human trafficking
intake_process: Call or text for confidential support, safety planning, and connection to local Michigan resources.
description: Michigan statewide hotline that connects people to support, information, and local service providers.

name: Michigan Crime Victim Compensation Program
eligibility: Crime victims in Michigan with qualifying out-of-pocket losses or expenses
intake_process: Apply through the state program or contact the office for application help and eligibility guidance.
description: State program that may help cover qualifying costs such as medical care, counseling, lost earnings, and other crime-related expenses.

name: Crime Victims Legal Assistance Project (CVLAP)
eligibility: Individuals in Michigan seeking free civil legal assistance related to domestic violence and certain other victimization matters
intake_process: Use the website to review eligibility and request legal assistance.
description: Statewide program providing free civil legal assistance to eligible survivors, including people affected by domestic violence.

name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
eligibility: Anyone in the United States seeking domestic violence support
intake_process: Call, chat online, or use text support for confidential help, safety planning, and referrals.
description: National confidential support line for people experiencing domestic violence or abuse.

name: RAINN National Sexual Assault Hotline
eligibility: Anyone in the United States impacted by sexual assault
intake_process: Call, chat, or text for confidential crisis support and referrals.
description: National hotline for survivors of sexual assault and their loved ones.

name: StrongHearts Native Helpline
eligibility: Native American and Alaska Native survivors of domestic and sexual violence
intake_process: Call or text for confidential and anonymous support and advocacy.
description: National helpline offering confidential, anonymous, culturally rooted support for Native survivors.

name: WomensLaw Michigan Resources
eligibility: Anyone seeking Michigan domestic violence legal information, advocates, shelters, and related help
intake_process: Use the Michigan resource page to find advocates, shelters, lawyers, courthouse information, and legal information.
description: Michigan-focused legal and referral resource for survivors looking for shelter, advocacy, and legal information.

name: Community Housing Network
address: 250 Elizabeth Lake Rd, Suite 1000, Pontiac, MI 48341
eligibility: Individuals and families in Oakland County experiencing housing instability or homelessness
intake_process: Call or visit the website to connect with a housing specialist. Walk-ins welcome during business hours.
description: Nonprofit connecting individuals and families to safe, stable, affordable housing and support services in Oakland County.

name: Southwest Housing Solutions
eligibility: Detroit and Wayne County residents seeking affordable housing or homeownership counseling
intake_process: Call or visit the website for available programs. Appointment recommended.
description: Affordable housing development and homeownership counseling organization serving Detroit communities.

name: Forgotten Harvest
hours: Mon–Fri: 8am–4pm; food rescue and distribution ongoing
eligibility: Open to anyone in need in metro Detroit; distribution sites vary by location
intake_process: Visit the website to find the nearest food distribution site. No appointment needed at most locations.
description: Rescues surplus food from grocery stores, restaurants, and farms and redistributes it to over 260 partner agencies across metro Detroit.

name: Food Gatherers
eligibility: Open to Washtenaw County residents experiencing food insecurity
intake_process: Visit the website to find a partner food pantry near you. Most pantries require no prior registration.
description: Washtenaw County's community food bank, coordinating food rescue and distribution through a network of local partner agencies.

name: Ann Arbor Area Meals on Wheels
address: 2573 S. State St, Ann Arbor, MI 48104
eligibility: Homebound individuals in Washtenaw County who have difficulty preparing meals
intake_process: Call to schedule an intake assessment. Meal delivery is typically within a few days of approval.
description: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound individuals throughout Washtenaw County, including Ann Arbor and Ypsilanti.

name: Jackets for Jobs
address: 150 W. Jefferson Ave, Suite 2500, Detroit, MI 48226
eligibility: Individuals preparing for job interviews or entering the workforce who need professional clothing
intake_process: Call or visit the website to schedule an appointment. A referral from a partner agency such as Michigan Works! may be requested.
description: Provides free professional clothing and accessories to individuals preparing for job interviews and employment in the Detroit metro area.

name: The Salvation Army – Metro Detroit
eligibility: Open to all in need; some emergency assistance programs have income guidelines
intake_process: Walk in to a service center or call for emergency assistance. Thrift store clothing available without eligibility requirements.
description: Provides emergency food, clothing, disaster relief, shelter, and social services across metro Detroit through multiple locations.

name: Cass Community Social Services
hours: Mon–Fri: 9am–5pm; meal programs have additional hours
eligibility: Open to all individuals in need, especially those experiencing homelessness or extreme poverty
intake_process: Walk in during business hours for most services. Call for information about housing and case management programs.
description: Comprehensive social services hub offering hot meals, clothing, showers, laundry, housing support, and employment programs in Detroit.

name: Detroit Rescue Mission Ministries
hours: Daily services; call or check website for specific program hours
eligibility: Open to individuals in need, including those experiencing homelessness
intake_process: Walk in to any program location. Call ahead for residential programs and case management appointments.
description: Provides emergency shelter, hot meals, showers, hygiene supplies, substance use recovery, and job training for individuals in Detroit.

name: Starfish Family Services
eligibility: Families with children from birth through age 18 in Wayne County
intake_process: Call or visit the website to learn about available programs and begin enrollment.
description: Provides early childhood education, family support, mental health services, and crisis intervention for families in Wayne County.

name: The Guidance Center
eligibility: Children, youth, adults, and families in Downriver Wayne County communities
intake_process: Call to schedule an intake appointment or speak with a staff member about your needs.
description: Behavioral health and family support organization serving Downriver communities with counseling, crisis services, and parenting programs.

name: SMART – Suburban Mobility Authority for Regional Transportation
hours: Bus routes operate daily; Customer service Mon–Fri: 7am–7pm
eligibility: Open to all riders; reduced fare programs available for seniors, people with disabilities, and low-income riders
intake_process: Plan your trip using the website or call customer service. Purchase passes at RideStore locations or online.
description: Regional bus service connecting suburban communities in Wayne, Oakland, and Macomb counties with routes into Detroit.

name: DDOT – Detroit Department of Transportation
hours: Bus routes operate daily; Customer service Mon–Fri: 8am–5pm
eligibility: Open to all riders; reduced fare programs available for seniors, people with disabilities, and youth
intake_process: Plan routes using the DDOT website. Purchase passes at RideStore locations, online, or through the Token Transit app.
description: City of Detroit's public bus system, operating routes throughout Detroit and connecting with SMART suburban service.

name: Vista Maria
address: 20651 W. Warren Ave, Dearborn Heights, MI 48127
eligibility: Girls, young women, and LGBTQ+ youth ages 10–25 in need of residential services, crisis intervention, or trauma support
intake_process: Call for intake assessment or crisis placement. Referrals accepted from courts, DHHS, and self-referrals.
description: Residential treatment, crisis intervention, behavioral health, and reentry support for girls and young women affected by trauma, abuse, and trafficking.

name: Mariners Inn
eligibility: Men 18 and older seeking substance use recovery services and transitional housing in Detroit
intake_process: Call for an intake appointment or walk in during business hours. Services available to men who are ready to begin recovery.
description: Long-term residential recovery program providing substance use treatment, job training, housing, and reintegration support for men in Detroit.

name: Hegira Health
hours: Mon–Fri: 8am–5pm; crisis services available extended hours
eligibility: Adults and youth in Wayne, Monroe, and surrounding counties seeking mental health or substance use services
intake_process: Call to schedule an intake appointment at the nearest location. Walk-ins accepted for crisis services.
description: Community mental health and substance use recovery organization serving adults and youth across Wayne, Monroe, and Washtenaw counties.

name: Henry Ford Maplegrove Center
address: 6773 W Maple Rd, West Bloomfield, MI 48322
eligibility: Adults seeking addiction recovery, detox, and behavioral health support
intake_process: Call or visit the website for admission requirements and program details
description: One of Michigan’s most established addiction centers offering full-spectrum care (detox → rehab → aftercare) with a strong medical and psychiatric approach.

name: Meridian Health Services – Detox & Men's Residential
address: 1255 N Oakland Blvd, Waterford Township, MI 48327
eligibility: Men seeking detox and residential addiction treatment
intake_process: Call for admission details and residential intake
description: Detox and men’s residential addiction care at a community-oriented treatment center serving Oakland County.

name: Meridian Health Services – Women's Residential
address: 1435 N Oakland Blvd, Waterford Township, MI 48327
eligibility: Women seeking residential addiction treatment
intake_process: Call for admission details and residential intake
description: Women’s residential addiction treatment at Meridian Health Services, offering structured recovery and supportive care.

name: Meridian Health Services – Outpatient & MAT
eligibility: Individuals seeking outpatient therapy and medication-assisted treatment for opioid use disorder
intake_process: Call for outpatient intake and MAT enrollment
description: Provides outpatient therapy, medication-assisted treatment, and recovery planning across multiple levels of care.

name: Sacred Heart Rehabilitation Center
eligibility: Individuals seeking affordable addiction treatment and telehealth services
intake_process: Call or use the website to connect with admissions and program options
description: A long-standing nonprofit provider offering affordable, statewide addiction services, including telehealth and multiple program levels.

name: Oakland Community Health Network (OCHN)
eligibility: Oakland County residents seeking publicly funded substance use disorder services
intake_process: Visit the website or call to access prevention, treatment coordination, and recovery referrals
description: Oversees and funds substance use disorder services across Oakland County, connecting residents to treatment providers and recovery resources.

name: Macomb County Community Mental Health (MCCMH)
address: 43740 N Groesbeck Hwy, Clinton Township, MI 48036
eligibility: Medicaid, uninsured individuals, and residents seeking substance use support
intake_process: Call or visit the website to learn about treatment, prevention, and recovery programs
description: Provides county-based access to addiction treatment, especially for individuals with limited financial resources.

name: University of Michigan Addiction Center
address: 750 N University Ave, Ann Arbor, MI 48104
eligibility: Adults and families seeking academic addiction care and psychiatric treatment
intake_process: Call or visit the website to schedule an appointment or learn about clinical programs
description: A specialized academic center combining treatment, research, and training in addiction medicine and psychiatry.

name: Michigan Department of Health & Human Services (MDHHS) Treatment Directory
eligibility: Michigan residents seeking treatment referrals and public addiction services
intake_process: Use the website or helpline to find licensed providers, crisis lines, and treatment guidance
description: The central hub for finding licensed treatment providers and accessing public addiction services in Michigan.

name: WHEAT Organization
eligibility: Parents and caregivers seeking family support, childcare information, and education resources
intake_process: Visit the website to learn about support programs, referral services, and family resources.
description: Online family support organization connecting parents to childcare, early learning, and health resources.

name: Great Start to Quality
eligibility: Families searching for childcare, preschool, and early learning resources in Michigan
intake_process: Use the website or call to find quality childcare and preschool programs in your area.
description: Connects families to childcare, preschool, and parenting resources across Michigan.

name: Early On Michigan
hours: Mon–Fri 8am–5pm; phone support outside business hours may be available
eligibility: Children birth to age 3 and their families seeking developmental support
intake_process: Call or visit the website for a free screening and to connect with local Early On services.
description: Free developmental support for children 0–3. If you’re worried about your child’s development, this is free and does not require insurance.

name: Healthy Start
eligibility: Pregnant people, infants, and families seeking home visiting and parenting support
intake_process: Visit the MDHHS website to learn about Healthy Start home visiting and parenting education programs.
description: Healthy Start offers home visits, parenting education, and pregnancy and infant support through Michigan DHHS.

name: Diaper Bank of Southeastern Michigan
eligibility: Families in need of diapers and baby supplies in Southeastern Michigan
intake_process: Contact a partner agency or visit the website to learn how to access free diapers through partner sites.
description: Provides free diapers through partner agencies; families usually access services through partner sites rather than direct pickup.

name: Detroit Diaper Bank
eligibility: Detroit families needing diapers, wipes, and baby supplies
intake_process: Visit the website for current distribution events and partner agency information.
description: Provides diapers, wipes, and baby supplies for Detroit-based families needing immediate material support.

name: Maternal Infant Health Program (MIHP)
eligibility: Medicaid pregnant individuals and infants
intake_process: Visit the website to find MIHP contact information and enroll in in-home services.
description: MIHP is Michigan’s statewide home visiting program for Medicaid-eligible pregnant individuals and infants, providing coordinated in-home services.

name: Judson Center
eligibility: Families seeking parenting classes, foster/adoption support, and behavioral health services
intake_process: Visit the website or call to learn about program availability at local locations.
description: Provides parenting classes, foster and adoption support, and behavioral health services at locations in Royal Oak, Detroit, and Warren.

name: Pregnancy Resource Center Lincoln Park
eligibility: Adults, young adults, teens, pregnant people, families, and those in crisis
intake_process: Call or visit the website to access confidential pregnancy support, baby supplies, and one-on-one services.
description: Pregnancy Center Lincoln Park offers confidential support, prenatal care, baby supplies, diapers, formula, and emergency food for families in crisis.

name: The Cloth Option
eligibility: Caregivers in need of cloth diaper loans and reusable hygiene products
intake_process: Apply online to receive cloth diaper loans and reusable hygiene supplies based on child age.
description: Distributes cloth diaper loans and reusable hygiene products to caregivers in need, with age-based diaper allowances for newborns, infants, and toddlers.

name: Gianna House Community Outreach Center
eligibility: Mothers and pregnant people seeking prenatal education, support groups, and baby supplies
intake_process: Call or visit the website to learn about upcoming support groups, prenatal classes, and community services.
description: Offers prenatal education, support groups for moms, baby supplies, and community programs in Eastpointe.

name: MedSource
eligibility: Patients and caregivers seeking medical equipment, durable medical supplies, and infant care products
intake_process: Call or visit the website to order DME products and learn about delivery options.
description: Offers durable medical equipment and baby care products, including specialty formula, breast pumps, mobility aids, and medical supplies.

name: LAHC MIH Community Clinic
hours: Call or visit the website for hours and appointment details
eligibility: Individuals and families seeking community-based primary and integrated health care services
intake_process: Visit the website or call the clinic to schedule an appointment and learn about available services.
description: Community health clinic providing integrated and primary care services in Dearborn, MI.

name: Say Detroit Clinic
hours: Call or visit the website for appointment hours
eligibility: People seeking reproductive health services, including family planning and sexual health care
intake_process: Contact the clinic by phone or website to make an appointment and learn about available services.
description: Reproductive health clinic offering family planning, STI testing, and sexual health services in Highland Park.

name: The Luke Clinic
hours: Call or visit the website for service hours
eligibility: People seeking reproductive, abortion, or pregnancy-related health services
intake_process: Call the clinic or use the website to learn about available services and schedule a visit.
description: Reproductive health clinic in Detroit offering pregnancy, abortion, and women's health services.

name: Detroit Association Of Black Organizations (DABO)
hours: Call or visit the website for appointment and testing hours
eligibility: Young people ages 13 to 30 seeking free HIV testing and health navigation services
intake_process: Call or visit the website to schedule free HIV testing and connect with health navigation support.
description: Free HIV testing and health navigation for youth ages 13 to 30 through the Detroit Association Of Black Organizations.

name: Western Wayne Family Health Centers
address: Online resource; multiple Michigan clinic locations
hours: Call for appointment and service hours
eligibility: Patients with Medicaid/Healthy Michigan plans or uninsured individuals needing sliding-scale women's health care
intake_process: Call or use the website to schedule OB-GYN care, prenatal visits, and reproductive health services.
description: Accepts Medicaid/Healthy Michigan and offers sliding scale OB-GYN and reproductive health services for uninsured patients.

name: Summit Medical Centers
hours: Call or visit the website for appointment availability
eligibility: Women and girls seeking abortion services and reproductive care
intake_process: Call or visit the website to learn about available abortion methods and schedule a consultation.
description: Provides abortion services and reproductive health care to women and girls based on individual needs and available resources.

name: DCHC HIV/AIDS Program
hours: Call for hours and program details
eligibility: People living with or at risk for HIV/AIDS who need medical care, prevention, and support services
intake_process: Call either number or visit the website to access HIV/AIDS care, sliding-scale services, and insurance navigation.
description: HIV/AIDS program offering care, prevention, and sliding-scale services with Medicaid/Medicare support for underinsured clients.

name: Women Veterans Health Care
hours: Call for appointment availability and service hours
eligibility: Women veterans seeking comprehensive, gender-specific VA health care services
intake_process: Call the VA facility or visit the website to access women veterans health care and schedule an appointment.
description: Women Veterans Health Care provides gender-specific, comprehensive services to veterans at Detroit VA facilities.

name: Vivent Health Medical Care
hours: Call or visit the website for service hours and appointment details
eligibility: People seeking HIV medical care, behavioral health, and wellness services
intake_process: Call or visit the website to connect with HIV medical care and wellness services in Highland Park.
description: Provides HIV medical care and health navigation services at Highland Park, Michigan.

name: Authentic Birth Doula Support
hours: Call or email for appointment information
eligibility: Pregnant people seeking doula support, Medicaid-covered prenatal and postpartum visits, and birth planning services
intake_process: Call or email to request more information and complete service agreements and waivers before services begin.
description: Provides Medicaid-friendly doula support with sliding-scale fees for pregnant people, including prenatal and postpartum care agreements.

name: Planned Choices Health Services
hours: Call or visit the website for appointment and clinic hours
eligibility: People seeking family planning counseling, contraceptive care, and reproductive health services
intake_process: Call or visit the website to schedule services and learn about available counseling and care options.
description: Provides family planning counseling and reproductive health services in Allen Park, Michigan.

name: Trinity Health Michigan Virtual Care
hours: Online care available based on clinician availability
eligibility: Patients seeking virtual medical care for a variety of non-emergency conditions
intake_process: Visit the website to establish care and schedule virtual appointments with Trinity Health clinicians.
description: Virtual care offering safe, fast, and reliable medical services from the comfort of home.

name: CHASS Center Family Planning
hours: Call or visit the website for service hours
eligibility: All clients seeking family planning counseling, reproductive health care, and affordable contraceptive supplies
intake_process: Call the center or visit the website to access family planning counseling and reproductive health services.
description: Provides family planning counseling and reproductive health services for clients of all ages, including affordable contraceptive supplies.

name: Christie Donn Medicaid Doula Services
hours: Call or email for appointment details
eligibility: Pregnant people enrolled in Medicaid who want doula support during pregnancy and postpartum
intake_process: Call or visit the website to get more information and arrange Medicaid-covered doula visits.
description: Medicaid-covered doula services during pregnancy and up to 12 months postpartum, available without a referral.

name: AAA Pregnancy Information Services
hours: Call or visit the website for service hours
eligibility: Women, men, and families currently pregnant who need pregnancy support services
intake_process: Call or visit the website to access pregnancy support, counseling, and family services.
description: Supports pregnant women, men, and families with pregnancy information, counseling, and services in Livonia.

name: Michigan Department of Health & Human Services (MDHHS) – Cash & Food Assistance
eligibility: Low-income individuals and families
intake_process: Call the number or visit the website to apply for SNAP, TANF, or state emergency relief.
description: Provides SNAP food stamps, TANF cash assistance, and state emergency relief for rent and utilities.

name: WIC – Wayne County
eligibility: Pregnant women, new mothers, infants, and children up to age 5 with income guidelines
intake_process: Call the local office or visit the website to apply for WIC benefits.
description: Provides formula, food benefits, breastfeeding support, and nutrition education for eligible families.

name: Michigan Child Development & Care (CDC) Subsidy
eligibility: Low-income working parents or students
intake_process: Visit the website to learn about eligibility and apply for childcare subsidy.
description: Helps pay for childcare costs for low-income working parents or students.

name: Head Start (Wayne County)
eligibility: Children ages 3-5 from low-income families
intake_process: Visit the website or contact local Head Start programs to enroll.
description: Provides free preschool, early childhood development, meals, and family support services.

name: SMART ADA / Reduced Fare Programs
eligibility: Low-income individuals, disabled persons, and seniors
intake_process: Apply through SMART for reduced fare passes; Wayne Metro may provide bus passes or gas cards case-by-case.
description: Provides reduced fare passes for low-income, disabled, and senior riders; may include bus passes and gas cards.

name: Michigan Works! Reentry Programs
eligibility: Individuals reentering the community after incarceration
intake_process: Contact Michigan Works! for job placement and housing connections through reentry programs.
description: Offers job placement, housing connections, and probation support services for reentry.

name: AgeWays
eligibility: Seniors and older adults
intake_process: Visit the website to learn about senior services and support.
description: Provides services and support for seniors and older adults.

name: Michigan Rehabilitation Services (MRS)
eligibility: People with disabilities seeking job training
intake_process: Visit the website to apply for job training and rehabilitation services.
description: Provides job training and support for people with disabilities.

name: Disability Network Wayne County Detroit
eligibility: People with disabilities
intake_process: Visit the website for independent living support and advocacy services.
description: Offers independent living support and advocacy for people with disabilities.

name: St. Vincent de Paul – Clothing Assistance
eligibility: Individuals in need of clothing
intake_process: Contact St. Vincent de Paul for clothing assistance.
description: Provides clothing assistance through charitable services.

name: Salvation Army
address: Multiple locations in Detroit and Dearborn, MI
eligibility: Individuals in need
intake_process: Visit local Salvation Army locations for clothing and other assistance.
description: Offers clothing and other charitable assistance at multiple Detroit and Dearborn locations.

name: ACCESS Domestic Violence Program
eligibility: Victims of domestic violence
intake_process: Contact ACCESS for DV support, shelter connections, and culturally specific services.
description: Provides domestic violence support, shelter connections, and culturally specific services in Dearborn.

name: Charity Motors
eligibility: Individuals in need of vehicle assistance
intake_process: Call to learn about car purchase assistance programs.
description: Assists with purchasing cars for those in need.

name: The Last Mission Project
eligibility: Veterans with PTSD
intake_process: Visit the website to learn about financial support for PTSD treatment.
description: Provides financial support to alleviate stress for veterans seeking PTSD treatment.

name: CORE Gives
eligibility: Food and beverage service employees experiencing financial hardship due to illness or medical emergency
intake_process: Visit the website to apply for support for children during family medical crises.
description: Supports food and beverage service employees with children experiencing financial hardship due to illness or medical emergency.
